page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c2m1.large is an entry-level virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.e server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 virtual CPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core. It features a shared CPU allocation model and is managed via the KVM hypervisor.

This instance is equipped with 1.0 GB of system memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 0.5 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Due to its shared CPU allocation, low memory capacity, and lack of local storage, the ecs.e-c2m1.large is designed for budget-conscious, low-intensity workloads. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for basic utility tasks, lightweight testing, or simple background processes that do not require dedicated compute resources or high memory overhead.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e-c2m1.large is an entry-level virtual machine from the ecs.e family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 2 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.5 GHz with a shared CPU allocation model. The instance is configured with 1.0 GB of system memory, providing 0.5 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, bundled GPUs, or baseline network bandwidth. Given its shared resource model and minimal memory footprint, this instance represents a highly budget-focused tier. It is best suited for low-intensity workloads, basic utility scripts, lightweight development environments, and simple background tasks that do not demand dedicated compute performance or high memory capacity.
